title: Aluminum Price Reaches $3,503.05 USD/T Amid Supply Disruptions
summary: Aluminum prices rose to $3,503.05 USD/T on May 8, 2026, up 0.57% from the previous day, with a 44.69% increase year-over-year. The price surge is driven by prolonged supply disruptions from the Middle East due to the naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, while strong manufacturing activity from China supports demand.

title: Global Aluminium Production Declines in Q1 2026
summary: Global aluminium production slipped from 18.6 million tonnes in Q4 2025 to 18.3 million tonnes in Q1 2026, representing a 1.6% decline. This production decrease reflects ongoing market challenges and supply chain disruptions.

title: US Aluminum Industry Resilience Amid War Disruptions
summary: Current US domestic production capacity sits at approximately 660,000 metric tons annually, representing a dramatic decline from historical levels. The article examines the resilience of the US aluminum industry in the face of ongoing geopolitical disruptions.

title: Steel Price Falls Slightly but Rebar Futures Surge Post-Holiday
summary: Steel prices fell to 3,244 CNY/T on May 8, down 0.22% from the previous day, though up 5.63% over the past month and 6.40% year-over-year. Steel rebar futures surged to around CNY 3,250 per ton, the strongest since August last year, driven by robust buying in China after the May Day holiday and seven consecutive weeks of inventory drawdowns indicating tightening supply.

title: HRC Steel Price Dips Marginally Amid Recent Gains
summary: HRC Steel fell to 1,130 USD/T on May 8, down 0.09% from the previous day, after reaching 1,100 USD/T, the highest since January 2024. Over the past four weeks, prices gained 3.79%, reflecting ongoing strength in the market.

title: India's NMDC Raises Iron Ore Prices for Fourth Straight Month
summary: Indian state-controlled miner NMDC increased iron ore prices in May despite pressure on domestic steel prices from slow demand. The price for 10-40mm Baila 65.5% lump ore was set at 5,500 rupees/t. Meanwhile, Indian hot-rolled coil prices fell from a near three-year high due to labor shortages, tighter financing, and anticipated corrections.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The restart is anticipated to support ongoing market dynamics amid rising prices.

title: Copper futures remained above $5.95 per pound on Friday after rebounding
summary: Copper futures stayed above $5.95 per pound on Friday, rebounding in the previous session due to signs of improving demand in China. This occurred despite headwinds from the Middle East conflict. Prices rose to 6.25 USD/Lbs on May 8, up 1.98% from the previous day.

title: Own-sourced copper output from Glencore's African copper assets surges 68% year on year
summary: Glencore's African copper assets, KCC and Mutanda in the Democratic Republic of Congo, saw own-sourced copper output surge by 68% year on year to 67,900 tonnes. This increase highlights strong production growth amid global supply constraints. Access the full report for detailed figures.

title: China state firm discusses major new copper mine in Congo
summary: China Railway Group is proposing a major new copper mine in the Democratic Republic of Congo, anticipated to produce between 200,000 and 500,000 tons of copper annually. This development aims to boost copper supply from the region. The project is under discussion with state firms.

title: US imposes sanctions on Congo armed group, mining firms over illicit minerals
summary: The United States announced sanctions against an armed group and mining firms in the Democratic Republic of Congo involved in illicit minerals trade, potentially impacting local copper production. This targets disruptions in the supply chain from the region. The sanctions were issued on Tuesday.

title: Freeport delays Grasberg full restart to early 2028
summary: Freeport-McMoRan has delayed the full restart of its Grasberg mine in Indonesia to early 2028, affecting copper production capacity. The mine has been operating below capacity following a fatal mudslide that triggered a force majeure. This postponement exacerbates global supply uncertainties.

title: In Indonesia, the Grasberg mine continues to operate below capacity following a fatal mudslide
summary: The Grasberg mine in Indonesia remains below full production capacity due to a fatal mudslide that led to a force majeure declaration. This ongoing issue contributes to tighter global copper supply. Production challenges persist amid other supply disruptions.

title: Powering the 21st Century with American Copper
summary: A video discussing American copper production and its role in powering modern infrastructure and technology in the 21st century.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: Explores copper's critical role in enabling a net-zero energy future by efficiently transmitting electricity and minimizing energy losses in sustainable energy infrastructure.

title: Nickel Prices Today 2026 | Live Chart, Battery Grade & Stainless Steel
summary: Global nickel markets are trading flat with prices holding steady after surging to multi-year highs. Nickel edged up 0.19% to $19,127.31/Ton globally and ¥130,526/Ton in China, driven by supply constraints from Indonesia's mining quota cuts and a global sulfur shortage due to Middle East tensions. Bullish momentum is supported by a weaker US dollar and firm SHFE supplier quotes.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel fell to 18,945 USD/T on May 8, down 0.89%, after pulling back from a two-year high near $19,600 due to profit-taking. Despite the dip, prices are up 10.05% over the past month and 19.53% year-over-year, underpinned by Indonesia's mining quota cuts and a sulfur shortage from Strait of Hormuz disruptions. Futures traded around $19,000 per tonne amid strong fundamentals.

title: Freeport now aims for 65% capacity in the second half of 2026, 80% by mid-2027, and near full capacity by the end of 2027
summary: Freeport has updated its production ramp-up targets for nickel operations, aiming for 65% capacity utilization in the second half of 2026, increasing to 80% by mid-2027 and nearing full capacity by the end of 2027. This reflects ongoing efforts to expand nickel output amid global demand pressures. The announcement highlights challenges and timelines in achieving higher production levels.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel prices fell to 18,945 USD/T on May 8, 2026, down 0.89% from the previous day, amid reports on production capacity and market trends. Over the past month, prices have risen 10.05%, indicating volatility tied to supply dynamics. The data underscores ongoing global nickel production and capacity influences on pricing.

title: Tenaris to Acquire Artrom Steel Tubes S.A.
summary: Tenaris has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire 100% of the share capital of Artrom Steel Tubes S.A. from GLGH Steel, LLC, a U.S.-based company. The acquisition is expected to expand Tenaris's industrial pipe product range and manufacturing footprint, strengthening its ability to serve customers.

title: Gabriel Podskubka Appointed as Chief Executive Officer
summary: Tenaris's Board of Directors has appointed Gabriel Podskubka as Chief Executive Officer. Paolo Rocca will continue to serve as Chairman of the Board.

title: Tenaris Awards Roberto Rocca Scholarships to Saudi Engineering Students
summary: Tenaris has awarded three Roberto Rocca Scholarships to engineering students from King Fahd University of Petroleum & Minerals in Saudi Arabia. This reinforces the company's commitment to developing local engineering talent in the Kingdom.

title: 2025 Sustainability Statement Published
summary: Tenaris has published an executive summary of its 2025 Sustainability Statement included in its annual report. The statement outlines how sustainability is embedded in the company's industrial strategy amid a complex geopolitical and market environment.
